stem: In Oracle a NULL value means that the value is not known.
explanation: In Oracle, NULL means the value is unknown, missing, or not applicable. It is not zero and not an empty string, which is why NULL = NULL does not evaluate to true and you must use IS NULL.

stem: Which order of correct hierarchy from the largest to the smallest from the list below:
explanation: The data hierarchy from largest to smallest is file > record > field: a file contains many records, and each record contains several fields (which in turn contain characters). Only D lists them in that order.

stem: What is the correct answer when using AND gate true table in the digital circuits design:
explanation: An AND gate outputs 1 only when every input is 1, so 1 AND 1 = 1. Note options A and C print the same false claim, and 1 AND 0 = 0, not 1.

stem: What is the correct answer when using OR gate true table in the digital circuits design:
explanation: An OR gate outputs 1 when at least one input is 1, so 0 OR 1 = 1. Every other option inverts a row of the truth table (1 OR 0 = 1, 1 OR 1 = 1, 0 OR 0 = 0).

stem: The following is an editor in UNIX:
explanation: vi is the standard UNIX visual editor, present on essentially every UNIX system, so D is the only real editor name listed. 'iv' is a letter-swap trap on vi, and 'edit' and 'editor' are not the names of the standard UNIX editor (the classic line editor is ed, and its screen version is vi).

stem: In computer languages, 17 mod 5 is:
explanation: mod returns the remainder of integer division: 17 = 3 * 5 + 2, so 17 mod 5 = 2. The distractor 3 is the quotient, not the remainder.

stem: The decimal conversion for the binary number 1100010 is:
explanation: Bit weights from the left are 64, 32, 16, 8, 4, 2, 1, so 1100010 = 64 + 32 + 2 = 98. The distractor 122 is a different bit pattern entirely (122 = 1111010).

stem: Long distance (over 2000 km) HF radio communication systems make use of:
explanation: HF signals (3 to 30 MHz) are refracted back to earth by the ionosphere, so sky wave propagation carries them thousands of kilometres beyond the horizon. Surface waves attenuate quickly with distance, and space waves are line of sight and limited by the horizon.

stem: Which one of the following is not a network layer:
explanation: The seven OSI layers are Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation and Application. 'Communication' is not one of them, so it is the odd one out.

stem: Which one of the following is used to display the configuration settings for the host:
explanation: ipconfig (ifconfig or ip on UNIX) prints the host's own configuration: IP address, subnet mask and default gateway. ping only tests reachability of another host, route shows the routing table, and SNMP is a management protocol rather than a host command.

stem: The IP address consists of two parts:

options.label: C
options.text: MAC Address ID and Host ID

explanation: The subnet mask splits an IP address into a network ID (which network the host is on) and a host ID (which machine on that network). The MAC address is a separate layer 2 identifier and is not part of the IP address.

stem: What are the phases of a project life cycle in order?

options.label: A
options.text: Define the project, Plan the project, Get approval/decision to proceed, Implement the project plan and evaluate the project.

options.label: B
options.text: Plan the project, Get approval/ decision to proceed, define the project, Implement the project plan and evaluate the project.

options.label: C
options.text: Evaluate the project, Get approval/ decision to proceed, Plan the project, Define the project and Implement the project plan.

explanation: The order is define, plan, get approval, implement, evaluate: you cannot plan what is not yet defined, approval gates spending on implementation, and evaluation only makes sense after delivery. B plans before defining and C evaluates before the project exists.

stem: A Terabyte is less than:
explanation: 1 PB = 1024 TB, so a terabyte is less than a petabyte. Every other option is smaller than a TB: 1000 MB is about 1 GB, 100 GB is 0.1 TB, and one million KB is also about 1 GB.

stem: A computer language used widely in developing website pages is:
explanation: HTML is the markup language browsers parse to render web pages, so it is the only web-page language listed. Pascal, Assembly and C are general-purpose or low-level languages with no role in authoring page content.

stem: Which one of the following is not a network topology:
explanation: A topology describes how nodes are physically or logically laid out (bus, ring, star). TCP/IP is a protocol suite, not a layout, so it is the odd one out. The stem is loose (Ethernet and ATM are strictly technologies too), but each is tied to a layout - Ethernet to bus/star, Token Ring to a ring - while TCP/IP is not.

stem: An internal website that is partially accessible to authorized outsiders is called:
explanation: An extranet is an intranet extended so that authenticated outsiders (suppliers, partners, contractors) can reach part of it. The trap is Intranet: that is the same site with no external access at all, and Internet is fully public.

stem: What does "plug and play" mean?

options.label: A
options.text: To get systems to work, users must first plug circuit boards into "play" slots on the motherboard.

options.label: B
options.text: This is a term used to describe how easy the newest generation of computers are to "play"

options.label: C
options.text: This is the term used to describe the various slots on the back of a system unit where the user "plugs in" devices in order to play games.

options.label: D
options.text: It means that the buyer of a new PC should be able to get the system up and running with a minimum of technical knowledge, installation, and delay.

options.label: E
options.text: Plug and play is a term coined by IBM to denote whether a computer device is "Internet-ready".

explanation: Plug and play means the system auto-detects and configures a device on connection, so the buyer gets the machine running with minimal technical knowledge, installation and delay. The other options are jokes built on the literal words 'plug' and 'play'.

stem: A minor upgrade of a software program is called a:
explanation: By the standard IT-literacy convention, a major upgrade produces a new version, while a minor upgrade is called a release. 'Preliminary test version' would be a beta, which is not what a minor upgrade is.

stem: What is the desktop software that is used to create presentations?
explanation: PowerPoint is the presentation application in MS Office. Access is a database, Project is project scheduling, and Java is a programming language.

stem: What are two purposes of segmenting a network with a bridge?
explanation: A bridge splits a LAN into separate segments, so fewer stations contend for the same wire and collisions drop. Note the stem asks for two purposes but only one valid option exists: C is backwards (a bridge increases the number of collision domains, it does not reduce them) and D is false because a bridge forwards broadcasts, so it does not create extra broadcast domains - only a router or VLAN does that.

stem: The Telnet protocol is used:

options.label: A
options.text: To communicate with a remote login service

options.label: B
options.text: To connect to multi-user computers or servers

explanation: Telnet is a remote-login protocol that opens a terminal session on a remote multi-user host or server, so A and B describe the same use from two angles. When two options are both true, 'Both of the above' is the answer.

stem: In Unix servers copying data to a magnetic tape takes the same time as copying the same data to a disk:
explanation: False. Magnetic tape is a sequential-access medium and must stream (and seek/rewind) through the media, while disk is random-access; writing the same data to tape is far slower than to disk.

stem: The current use of operating system, application programs and data are kept in:
explanation: Code and data that are currently in use must live in RAM (main memory), the only storage the CPU can fetch from at execution speed. The CPU itself holds only a few words in registers and cache, not the OS and programs.

stem: A Full Backup of your system will:

options.label: A
options.text: Duplicate all files created or changed since the last full backup

options.label: B
options.text: Stores all files from hard disk onto a tape or a disk.

options.label: C
options.text: Duplicate all files created or changed since the last backup of any type.

options.label: D
options.text: Guarantee that the system will not crash.

options.label: E
options.text: Should only be done once a month because it is time consuming.

explanation: A full backup copies every file from the hard disk to tape or disk, regardless of whether it changed. The classic traps: A describes a differential backup (changes since the last full backup) and C describes an incremental backup (changes since the last backup of any type).

stem: What is a Distributed System?

options.label: A
options.text: A collection of processors that do not share memory or a clock

options.label: B
options.text: A system that consists of many computers at different locations

options.label: C
options.text: A system that is distributed over more than one server

explanation: The textbook definition (Silberschatz) is a collection of processors that share neither memory nor a clock, each with its own local memory, communicating over a network. B and C describe consequences (many machines, many servers) rather than the defining property of no shared memory and no shared clock.
